name: Chocolate Protein Donut Holes
description: Glazed chocolate protein timbits, synthesized from three source recipes (a 45-ball cookies&cream air-fryer dough, a 26-ball oxywhey air-fryer dough, and an 8-ball baked egg+pudding dough). Best-of: the EGG + SF pudding from the baked version (the anti-rubber, anti-dry levers - fat for tenderness, gums to hold moisture), the real cocoa level + Greek-yogurt body + quick air-fry from the air-fryer versions (chocolatey and hard to overbake), AP flour + baking powder instead of drying coconut flour, and just ONE scoop of protein (two scoops is what turns these rubbery - more protein powder is NOT free here). Chocolate protein (out of vanilla). The glaze is the point: no powdered-sugar sub on hand, so granulated Splenda is blitzed to a powder for body, a little melted semi-sweet chocolate gives gloss and lets it actually SET in the fridge, and liquid monk fruit + a pinch of salt + espresso powder are the kick - concentrated sweetness and deepened chocolate with no grit. ~16 balls, very low fat/ball, high protein.

steps.instructionText: Whisk together the dry ingredients: AP flour, chocolate protein powder, cocoa, SF vanilla pudding mix, baking powder (1 tsp), granulated Splenda, and a pinch of salt.
Add the Greek yogurt and the whole egg; stir and fold to a soft, slightly sticky dough. Chill 15-20 min until it firms up enough to handle (protein/yogurt dough is sticky warm).
Roll into ~16 even balls with damp or lightly cocoa-dusted hands.

Air fry at 350F for ~5-6 min (or bake at 350F for 10-12 min) until just set and springy to a light press. Pull them the moment they spring back - do NOT chase a dry center, that's where they turn rubbery. Cool ~10 min.

Glaze: blitz the granulated Splenda to a fine powder in a blender or spice grinder (this is your DIY powdered sweetener - the body of the glaze). Separately, melt the semi-sweet chocolate chips in short microwave bursts, stirring, until just smooth.

Whisk the melted chocolate with the powdered Splenda, cocoa, almond milk, liquid monk fruit, vanilla, espresso powder, and a pinch of salt into a smooth, pourable glaze. Thin with a few more drops of almond milk if it's too thick to coat. Taste and add a drop more monk fruit if you want more kick.

Dip or toss the balls in the glaze while they're warm but NOT hot (warm grabs the glaze; hot makes it slide right off). Set on a wire rack and chill to set the glaze - the melted chocolate firms it into a real coating. Expect about half the glaze to drip off; that's normal.

notes.text: Overbaking is the #1 thing that ruins these. Protein-powder + Greek-yogurt dough goes rubbery and dry the second it overcooks. Air fryers run hot and fast - start checking at 5 min and pull the moment they spring back to a light press; the center can look slightly underdone and it'll be perfect once cooled.

The egg, the SF pudding mix, and holding protein to ONE scoop are the anti-rubber levers. Don't 'boost the macros' by adding a second scoop - extra whey is exactly what makes protein bakes dry and bouncy. If you want more protein in the day, eat two donut holes, don't load the dough.

No powdered-sugar sub on hand? Blitz the granulated Splenda to a fine powder in a blender or spice grinder - that powder is what gives the glaze body and a smooth (not grainy) coat. The little bit of melted semi-sweet chocolate is what makes it glossy and lets it SET in the fridge into a real shell instead of a wet smear.

The glaze kick comes from concentration, not sugar: liquid monk fruit dials sweetness up with zero grit, a pinch of salt sharpens it, and the espresso powder deepens the chocolate (you won't taste coffee at 1/8 tsp). Build the glaze, then taste and add monk fruit a drop at a time until it pops.
Chill the dough 15-20 min before rolling - warm protein/yogurt dough is sticky and won't ball up cleanly. Damp or lightly cocoa-dusted hands keep it from gluing to you.
